events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a field-service visit where the observed condition is "aux heat locked on" for thermostats & sequences in HVAC-R scope. This is a defect - auxiliary heat is locked on when it shouldn't be, indicating a thermostat or sequence of operation problem. This is an installed-defective condition. The work is at tested/inspected stage.

Aux heat locked on means the system is calling for auxiliary heat continuously, which could be a thermostat issue (staging, wiring, configuration) or a control board/sequencer issue. This is a defect that wastes energy and could indicate a failed component.

Decision: fail (non-compliant/defective condition)
Risk: medium to high - aux heat locked on wastes energy, can cause high bills, potential overheating issues
Component conditions: installed-defective, possibly failed
Percent complete: since it's tested/inspected and found defective, maybe 80-90% of the service visit but the work itself failed inspection. I'll estimate around 70.

Let me write the JSON.

  "percent_complete": 70,

  "findings": [
    "Auxiliary heat is locked on continuously, indicating a thermostat staging/sequence-of-operation fault or failed control component",
    "Continuous aux heat call wastes energy and can cause excessive cycling/overheating of electric heat elements",
    "Thermostat wiring, O/B terminal configuration, staging parameters, or heat sequencer/control board may be defective or misconfigured"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Verify thermostat wiring and O/B/reversing valve configuration against OEM manual",
    "Check thermostat staging/setup parameters and outdoor temperature lockout settings per manufacturer instructions",
    "Inspect heat sequencer/aux heat control contacts and control board outputs for stuck-closed or failed relay",
    "Confirm correct sequence of operation: compressor/primary heat should stage before aux heat unless outdoor lockout is satisfied",
    "Re-test thermostat call logic and defrost/aux heat staging after correction"
  ],
  "rationale": "Aux heat locked on is a sequence-of-operation defect: the thermostat or controls are continuously calling auxiliary heat instead of staging it properly. Per OEM service manuals, aux heat should only energize on second-stage call or during defrost/low outdoor ambient conditions. A stuck or misconfigured thermostat/sequencer constitutes an installed-defective, failed condition requiring correction before acceptance.",
  "references": [
    "mfr manuals - thermostat setup and staging configuration",
    "mfr manuals - sequence of operation for heat pump / aux heat",
    "mfr manuals - control board and heat sequencer troubleshooting"
  ]
}
